questions.stem: Which of the following best describes a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA)?

options.text: An abstract machine that accepts or rejects strings by transitioning through states based on input symbols
A database management system for storing automata
A type of programming language compiler
A network protocol for distributed computing

questions.explanation: A DFA is a theoretical model of computation that processes input strings symbol by symbol, transitioning between a finite set of states.

questions.stem: The class P is defined as the set of decision problems solvable in:

options.text: Polynomial time by a deterministic Turing machine

questions.explanation: P is the class of problems solvable in polynomial time on a deterministic Turing machine.

questions.stem: Which problem is known to be undecidable?

options.text: Finding the shortest path in a graph

questions.explanation: The Halting Problem was proven undecidable by Alan Turing — no algorithm can determine whether an arbitrary program will halt.
